rationale: Verified the appended refusal section word-for-word across all three surfaces: diffing `sed -n '/^## If approval refuses/,$p'` on .agents/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md vs .claude/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md yields zero diff, and the internal/setup/claude.go literal (lines 755-768) matches the same prose modulo Go string escaping. All three give identical recovery guidance: the refusal, `mindspec complete <bead>` as the recovery, the branch-less restoration-prerequisite recourse, and 'hatches do not bypass'. Pre-existing verb-order/step-5/marker drift outside the appended block (git push vs bd dolt push, mindspec approve impl vs impl approve, missing managed-by marker) confirmed present but correctly out of scope per BRIEF. `go build ./...` succeeds and `gofmt -l ./cmd ./internal` is empty. Inspected the appended literal's backtick code spans (`mindspec complete`, `mindspec impl approve <id>`, `bead/<id>`) — each follows the exact surrounding-file convention of closing/reopening the raw string via `` ` + "`text`" + ` `` with no shell single-quote escapes inside a doc comment, so the known gofmt doc-comment/backtick gotcha does not apply here (this is a string-literal map value, not a `//` doc comment). Worktree left clean.

findings.description: Appended refusal section is byte-identical (content-wise) across .agents/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md, and the internal/setup/claude.go embedded literal (escaping differences only, as expected for a Go raw string).

claude.go literal edit compiles cleanly (go build ./...) and is gofmt-clean (gofmt -l ./cmd ./internal empty); no backtick/shell-escape gotcha present since the edit is inside a string literal value, not a // doc comment.
Escaping convention (` + "`text`" + `) matches every other entry in lifecycleSkillFiles() verbatim; no deviation introduced.
